other_observers: Dan (John) Brooke.; We are original viewers. Later received emails from Glenn Walbek, who saw the bird at 8:30 AM (too far for photos) and from Mary and Dave Driscoll (who saw the bird at an undetermined time).
specific_location: Per resources on line Cherry Creek is at lat 39.3750, long -104.5045. We were viewing the bird from the floating dock area of the Lake Loop at the park, looking toward dam tower.
description: The bird was first identified by binoculars as a loon by its large size and low profile on the water, bill carried in a slightly upturned fashion. When we scoped it, in side profile the bird showed a dark back, gray head and neck with some fine stripes up the back of the neck, none were seen on the side of the neck. Red eye. As the bird turned to face us in direct sunlight, red throat obvious. The bird lacked side neck striping and lacked purple coloration that we have seen in breeding Pacific Loons in Alaska. Also didn't appear as "big headed" as Pac loon would, head appeared more delicate. When bird came up from diving, small white patch visible on flank.Behaviors: Bird sat low on the water, larger than nearby Western Grebes. Frequently engaged in behavior of looking below the water, whcih we have seen in diving birds, but much more in loons. Did actively engage in diving/feeding, and submerging itself under water.Call: Not heardPlumage: Alternate
